textPlain: Google is not down, half BGP routing table is gone. Lots of services are not working, but it has notighing to do with google.

Still is down - cannot access gmail, tried to Google the status page, then realised I'll have to use Bing for that.Surprised it still hasn't been resolved, the problems have been ongoing for 2 hours already.Waiting for that post mortem, really interested in how this failed - I'd assume they have dozens of fallback scenarios ready.
Related Ask NH thread is at https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45124458

During college time (way before LLMs) I had been experienced Google searches with custom things like "site:" with working knowledge about what websites in my websites would be useful.But Google changed and sometimes now they don't even respect putting the search term between quotation. And they lost the battle to SEO crap and content farms. I went with ublacklist and spent too much time curating my blocklist (from my browsing and from other lists). Google was starting to decrease in quality for my as researcher and casual search too but not for simple things that most people search I felt like any search engine from the available list (bing, duckduck..etc) would give the average person all what they want with reasonable quality.I moved to Kagi in 2021 and has been happy user since then but I use search heavily and not an average person using the internet. So I am in the niche but with Google AI summary problems and their extensive ads and content farm and SEO I don't see the appeal of Google to average user too.If what you look into is famous website and walled garden + some Wikipedia and news articles then I think this can replaced by anything.Most of the generated content these days are on the walled garden platform and most of it is not searchable anyway.Not to mention the geopolitical reasons that many countries in the world would not want to continue their dependency on a US company (It doesn't matter if we agree on them or not).   Not to mention it is good to de-google as much as possible for privacy reasons.

De-googling is great, and I found Kagi be a superior product. However their politics aren’t good either. Being neutral and apolitical somehow makes giving money (albeit a small percentage) to Yandex. And that company is a supporter of the Russian state.No thanks.
